name: Egg Noodles with Pork, Eggplant and Oyster Sauce
recipeIngredient: 5 ounces boneless pork top loin, thinly sliced
3 ounces Italian eggplant or Japanese eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the boneless pork into half-inch strips. Diagonally slice (¼ inch thick) to tenderize.
2. Marinate pork for 30 minutes in a mixture of 1 tablespoon each of soy sauce and wine (Chinese rice wine, dry sherry, or dry vermouth).
3. Trim ends off eggplant and cut once lengthwise, then into quarter-inch half-moon slices. Soak in ice water with 1 teaspoon soy sauce.
4. Peel the two small potatoes and cut into half-inch dice. Place in a bowl; submerge in salty ice water.
5. Scrub carrots, trim ends, slice into quarter-inch ovals (diagonal).
6. Cut green onion: dice white part, cut green portion into 1-inch lengths.
7. Finely chop garlic and ginger.
8. Drain mushrooms and use the liquid for sauce if not too salty; add broth as needed.
9. Mix sauce ingredients starting with reserved mushroom liquid, set aside.
10. Heat oil in a wok:
- Stir fry pork for 3 minutes
- Add potato, carrot, eggplant (2-3 minutes)
- Add garlic, ginger, green onions (1 minute)
- Make well for sauce and add liquid; cook until thickened
- Stir in straw mushroom pieces and cooked noodles
11. Serve immediately.
MC Est 404 CALS, 12.7 G FAT 28.5% CFF.
Tip: Start cooking pasta just after pork is done. Used medium egg noodles -- dried, short, curly, about ¼ inch wide (1-1/2 cups dry measure). Tested June 10, 1997. Bob liked it more than I did at first but grew to like it. Preferred potato, eggplant, and carrot. Wanted pork to be more tender.
